authorization_policies.Description: Used to manage authorization related settings across the company.

authentication_method_policy.Description: The tenant-wide policy that controls which authentication methods are allowed in the tenant, authentication method registration requirements, and self-service password reset settings

license_state.state_reason: At least one service plan is provisioned successfully.

PermissionRiskLevelWeights.Description: Risk level weights are assigned based on the level of access granted by each permission.

PermissionVolume.Description: Over-permissioned applications/service principals represent an increased attack surface regardless of individual permission risk level.

MultiTenant.Description: Multi-tenant applications can be used across multiple organizations, increasing their attack surface.

ThirdPartyServicePrincipal.Description: Third-party service principals are owned by external organizations and do not fall under the same security policies as internal service principals.

PrivilegedRoles.Description: Service principals with privileged roles (e.g., Global Administrator) have elevated permissions and pose a higher risk.

CredentialContextWeights.Description: Credential base points dynamically scale by the highest risk level permission on the app/SP.

CredentialTypeDiscounts.Description: Multiplier applied to credential and base points by credential type. Passwords hold the highest risk, then certificates, and federated creds with the least.

CredentialVolume.Description: Multiple active credentials increase the authentication attack surface. Each active credential beyond the first adds bonus points.

default_app_management_policy.Description: Default tenant policy that enforces app management restrictions on applications and service principals. To apply policy to targeted resources, create a new policy under appManagementPolicies collection.
